commands.review_summary:
The branch threads a command session-metadata-change signal from auto-reply command/directive persistence into Gateway `sessions.changed` broadcasts, adds `/goal`/directive/active-run tests, and documents command-only session refreshes.

PR surface: Source +295, Tests +545, Docs +2. Total +842 across 25 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main has `/goal` command store writes while the Gateway `sessions.changed` emitter is only reachable from RPC/lifecycle paths. I did not run a live repro in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should wait for contributor-supplied real behavior proof; there is no narrow ClawSweeper repair because the remaining blocker is environment evidence rather than a code defect.

target.title: Emit sessions.changed for in-chat command metadata
target.body:
Fixes #88598

## User-facing bug

Control UI clients subscribed to session updates could miss metadata changes made by in-chat commands. A command-only turn such as `/goal complete`, `/goal pause`, or `/goal clear` can update the session store without starting a model run, but the Gateway did not emit a `sessions.changed` event for that mutation. The visible session row could keep stale goal/status metadata until a manual reload or another unrelated session event.

## Exact repro

Source-level reproduction:

1. Connect a Control UI/WebChat client that subscribes to `sessions.changed`.
2. Send an in-chat `/goal` command that mutates the persisted session goal, for example `/goal complete shipped`.
3. The command handler writes the updated goal to the session store and returns command output.
4. Before this patch, no command-metadata `sessions.changed` event was broadcast for the store write, so subscribed clients did not get a scoped refresh for that session.

## Fix summary

- Add a typed `onSessionMetadataChanged` reply option for command handlers.
- Notify after successful command-session store writes through the shared `persistSessionEntry` helper.
- Notify `/goal` start, pause, resume, complete, blocked, clear, and status paths when they actually change the persisted goal metadata.
- Share the Gateway `sessions.changed` emitter between session RPC handlers and `chat.send`, so command-only chat turns can broadcast a scoped session row update.
- Delay command-metadata broadcasts until after the current command turn leaves the active-run map, while still preserving `hasActiveRun: true` if another run for the same session is active.
- Fall back to the shared session lifecycle event bus when command metadata changes happen outside `chat.send`, so raw channel dispatchers that use the same command-session store helper also refresh Gateway subscribers.
- Document that command-only session metadata changes refresh subscribed clients.

## Targeted tests

- Added `/goal` command unit coverage for metadata-change notifications on mutating goal actions and no-op clear paths.
- Added `/goal` command unit coverage for the shared lifecycle fallback when no direct Gateway subscriber callback is installed.
- Added Gateway sessions coverage for command-metadata `sessions.changed` payloads and active-run exclusion.
- Added Gateway chat coverage proving command-only `/goal` turns broadcast scoped session updates and do not report their own command turn as active.
- Added Gateway lifecycle coverage proving command metadata lifecycle events are broadcast to session subscribers.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: In-chat command metadata writes now emit scoped `sessions.changed` events, so subscribed Control UI/WebChat clients can refresh visible session metadata after command-only turns.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/sessions-changed-in-chat-commands`, commit `4b2c2f357077b1964dc97a4c823692ef4c808515`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `7c04ce3a79717437c28414ae39e6387283cf199d`. The Gateway proof starts a local WebSocket server/client pair and exercises the same `sessions.subscribe` plus `chat.send` event path used by Control UI/WebChat clients. The shared lifecycle fallback proof exercises command metadata notifications when no direct `chat.send` callback is available.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-goal.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "broadcasts command metadata lifecycle changes|chat.send broadcasts sessions.changed when in-chat command metadata changes|sessions.changed command metadata excludes only the command run from active state"`
- `git diff --check origin/main`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
- Auto-reply command metadata proof passed after the latest rebase: `Test Files 5 passed (5)`, `Tests 29 passed | 70 skipped (99)`. It verifies mutating goal/directive commands call `onSessionMetadataChanged({ sessionKey, reason: "command-metadata" })`, no-op clear paths do not notify, and the shared lifecycle fallback emits `reason: "command-metadata"` when no direct subscriber callback is installed.
- Gateway WebSocket/Control UI proof passed after the latest rebase: the targeted Gateway command passed with `Test Files 5 passed (5)`, `Tests 6 passed | 93 skipped (99)`. It subscribes with `sessions.subscribe`, sends `/goal clear` through `chat.send`, observes a `sessions.changed` event with `reason: "command-metadata"`, and verifies command metadata excludes only the command run while preserving active selected global runs.
- Shared sessions emitter proof passed in the same Gateway run. It verifies command metadata events exclude the command run itself while preserving `hasActiveRun: true` when another run for the same session is still active.
- Shared lifecycle fallback proof passed in the same Gateway run. It emits `emitSessionLifecycleEvent({ sessionKey, reason: "command-metadata", agentId })` and verifies subscribed clients receive the scoped `sessions.changed` payload with the refreshed session row.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` reported no actionable correctness issues in the command metadata notification and Gateway session-change broadcast paths. Its own Gateway WebSocket test attempt was blocked by sandbox `127.0.0.1` listen `EPERM`, not by a code failure; the elevated local-network Gateway proof above passed.

Observed result after fix: Command-only `/goal` metadata mutations produce scoped `sessions.changed` broadcasts with fresh session row metadata. `chat.send` keeps the command-only active-run exclusion, and non-`chat.send` command paths get the shared lifecycle fallback instead of leaving subscribers stale.

What was not tested: Full core typecheck is still blocked by pre-existing `ui/src/ui/views/workboard-3d-game.ts` errors for missing `three` type resolution and an implicit `any`. I did not run a live browser Control UI session against a real long-running `/goal` command. The Gateway WebSocket tests require local `127.0.0.1` listening; the default sandbox returned `listen EPERM`, so I reran the targeted Gateway proof with elevated local-network permission. I did not implement or test `/name` because this checkout does not contain a `/name` handler.

## Not tested / known gaps

- This patch focuses on command metadata changes that happen through `chat.send` reply options and the shared command-session store helper.
- Channel command flows that bypass the shared command-session store helper are unchanged.

## AI-assisted disclosure

This patch was prepared with AI assistance. I used source-level reproduction, targeted regression tests, and `codex review --base origin/main` to validate the behavior above.
